text: The patient is positioned laterally, and a camera port is inserted at the umbilicus. Two robotic 8mm ports are placed at the midclavicular line and anterior axillary line, along with a 5mm assistant port and a 12mm port for the liver retractor.

The liver is mobilized cephalically using a retractor. Laparoscopic intraoperative ultrasound guides the dissection of the adrenal vein near the inferior vena cava (IVC) and adrenal gland. The retroperitoneal covering is incised adjacent to the IVC, with camera magnification aiding in precise dissection despite limited space between the gland and the IVC.

The accessory adrenal vein is visualized and dissected using the harmonic scalpel. The surgeon controls the superior pedicle originating from the diaphragmatic branch with the harmonic scalpel. The inferior pedicle, originating from the superior polar branch of the renal artery, is also dissected with the harmonic scalpel. The inferior lateral attachments are dissected and divided.

The surgeon selectively dissects the accessory adrenal veins using an ultrasonic shear, achieving excellent hemostasis. The final dissection of the adrenal vein occurs at its junction with the inferior vena cava (IVC), where the vein is safely divided using the ultrasonic shear. The adrenal specimen is then placed into a catch bag.
